steps.title: Read circle R
steps.content:
A circle equation has the form:
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$
For circle $R$,
$$(x-4)^2+(y-7)^2=9$$
so the center is $(4,7)$ and the radius is $3$.

steps.title: Double the radius
steps.content:
Circle $s$ has the same center and twice the radius of circle $R$:
$$2(3)=6$$
The right side of the circle equation is the radius squared:
$$6^2=36$$

steps.title: Write the new equation
steps.content:
Circle $s$ is represented by:
$$(x-4)^2+(y-7)^2=36$$
The answer is B.
